title: epidendrum zosterifolium
intro: Epidendrum zosterifolium, also known as the False Zebra Orchid, is a species of orchid found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a terrestrial orchid with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as wound healing, antiseptic, and anti-arthritic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throat, and skin irritations.
